reviewed_findings.reason: dcat-ap-external-validation runs only on workflow_dispatch and release:published (no fork/PR trigger), and no privileged job restores this cache key (container.yml builds via docker build and never uses rust-cache). The rust-cache step is now restore-only (save-if: false), so this workflow cannot write a poisonable cache. zizmor still flags the presence of a caching action in a release-triggered workflow regardless of save-if; accepted as a mitigated, non-exploitable residual. Re-review if a privileged consumer of this cache key is introduced, or drop the rust-cache step to clear it entirely.

Not affected. Debian marks CVE-2023-45853 <ignored> for bookworm: the vulnerable contrib/minizip code is not built into the shipped zlib1g binary (no fixed package is coming, so the prior 'until the base image moves to a fixed distro package' exit condition was unreachable). The Relay binary's deflate path is pure-Rust (flate2/miniz_oxide/zlib-rs, no libz-sys C linkage) and the only `zip` crate is a dev-only XLSX test fixture, so MiniZip is never reached. zlib1g is a transitive base-image package only. Suppression retained to keep grype quiet; re-review when the base image leaves Debian bookworm.
